cases.title: Close-time absent finalization (AC-12) triggers BR-17 absence-threshold evaluation for enrolled students
preconditions: Testing-plan §3.1 Close-time absent finalization coverage block applies (FR-09, BR-13, AC-12).
Class section has effective policy absenceThresholdPercent 20 and excusedCountsTowardThreshold false.
Five closed sessions exist for the section; target student has four Absent records and one Present (80% unexcused absence rate).
M10 Notification module is enabled for the test environment.
steps: Open a sixth session, leave target student Pending, and invoke M03 closeSession transitioning beforeState Open to afterState Closed.
Verify BR-13 marks the pending student Absent, yielding five Absent of six countable sessions.
Invoke M10 evaluateAbsenceThreshold(classSectionId, studentUserId) post-close hook.
Query alert delivery queue or policy_alert_events table for the student-section pair.
cases.expected: Scenario-matrix cross-walk links AC-12 close-time absent finalization to FR-26: after session close recalculates unexcused absence rate (83.3% > 20% threshold) and M10 emits an informational absence-threshold alert per BR-17 without mutating attendance records.

cases.title: M10 absence-rate evaluator uses FR-25 effective policy absenceThresholdPercent at section scope
preconditions: Real DB transaction boundary for attendance_policies, attendance_records, and M10 alert persistence.
Institution default absenceThresholdPercent 30; section-scoped override absenceThresholdPercent 20 via attendance_policies row.
Class section with ten closed sessions: student has three Absent (30% rate — below institution default but above section 20%).
steps: Call M06 resolveEffectivePolicy(classSectionId, now) and confirm absenceThresholdPercent 20 sourced from ClassSection per BR-20.
Invoke M10 evaluateAbsenceThreshold(classSectionId, studentUserId).
Assert alert generated because 30% > 20% section threshold.
Repeat evaluation using institution-only policy (no section override, threshold 30%) and confirm no alert at 30% rate.
cases.expected: M10 integrates with M06 policy resolution: absence-threshold comparison uses FR-25 effective absenceThresholdPercent (section 20% wins over institution 30%); alert fires only when unexcused rate exceeds the resolved threshold.

cases.title: Excused absences excluded from threshold numerator when excusedCountsTowardThreshold is false
preconditions: Section policy: absenceThresholdPercent 20, excusedCountsTowardThreshold false.

Ten closed sessions for one enrolled student: two Absent, two Excused, six Present (unexcused rate 20% of ten total sessions, or 25% of eight countable if excused excluded from denominator — verify BR-17 formula).
M10 evaluator configured per domain model: Excused excluded from unexcused absence calculation when flag is false.
steps: Seed attendance_records with two Absent, two Excused, six Present across ten closed sessions.
Invoke M10 evaluateAbsenceThreshold(classSectionId, studentUserId).
Verify computed unexcusedAbsenceRate excludes Excused sessions from numerator and uses eligible session count as denominator.
Toggle excusedCountsTowardThreshold true on policy, re-run evaluation, and compare alert outcome.
cases.expected: When excusedCountsTowardThreshold false, Excused sessions are excluded from unexcused absence rate per FR-26 and BR-17; alert decision reflects only unexcused Absent sessions; toggling the flag changes the computed rate and may change alert eligibility.

cases.title: Session Open to Closed transition triggers post-close absence-threshold recalculation
preconditions: Open class session with one enrolled student remaining Pending.
Section policy absenceThresholdPercent 20; student already has two Absent of nine prior closed sessions (22.2% after next Absent).
M10 post-close hook subscribed to M03 session-close domain event.
steps: Capture beforeState: session Open, student Pending, no prior absence-threshold alert for this section.
Invoke closeSession(sessionId) transitioning session to Closed and applying BR-13 Absent for pending student.
Capture afterState: session Closed, student attendanceStatus Absent for this session.
Verify M10 evaluateAbsenceThreshold runs once post-close and persists alert event with computed rate and threshold snapshot.
cases.expected: beforeState Open → afterState Closed triggers BR-13 absent finalization then BR-17 threshold evaluation; alert event logged with unexcused rate exceeding 20% threshold; no attendance record statuses are auto-modified by the alert itself.

cases.title: Concurrent session closes for same section dedupe absence-threshold alerts to avoid noisy duplicates
preconditions: Section with absenceThresholdPercent 20; student already exceeds threshold before test.
Three sessions in Open state for the same class section affecting the same at-risk student.
M10 alert deduplication window or idempotency key on (classSectionId, studentUserId, policyVersion) is configured.
steps: Fire three parallel closeSession commands for distinct session IDs in the same section.
Collect all M10 alert events or notification queue rows for the student-section pair within a 60-second window.
Verify attendance_records reflect three new Absent rows (one per session) without duplication errors.
Assert at most one absence-threshold alert notification is emitted per deduplication policy.
cases.expected: Concurrent closes update attendance correctly while M10 deduplicates threshold alerts per MVP conditional scope ('avoid noisy false alarms'); student receives one informational alert, not three identical notifications for the same threshold breach state.

cases.title: POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/close emits absence-threshold alert auditable via GET /v1/audit-logs
preconditions: Section policy absenceThresholdPercent 20 configured via POST /v1/policies.
Student enrolled in section with four Absent of five prior closed sessions.
Sixth session is Open with the student Pending; Lecturer assigned to section is authenticated.
steps: Call POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/close with Lecturer Authorization and Idempotency-Key.
Assert HTTP 200 with data.state Closed.
Query GET /v1/audit-logs?actionType=AbsenceThresholdAlert&targetId={studentUserId}&from={closeTimestamp}.
Inspect audit payload: unexcusedAbsenceRate, absenceThresholdPercent, classSectionId, and recipient list metadata.
cases.expected: Session close HTTP 200 triggers BR-17 alert generation; audit log contains AbsenceThresholdAlert entry with computed rate above 20% threshold, student and section correlation IDs, and notification recipient configuration per FR-26; attendance records remain unchanged by the alert event.

cases.title: POST /v1/enrollments/import seeds roster then closed-session absences produce threshold breach alert
preconditions: AcademicAdmin authenticated; empty class section exists.
Section policy absenceThresholdPercent 20.
CSV enrollment rows for five students via POST /v1/enrollments/import; one row intentionally rejected for row-level error assertion.
steps: Call POST /v1/enrollments/import with four valid student rows and one invalid row (unknown studentCode).
Assert HTTP 200 with data.acceptedRows 4 and data.rejectedRows containing rowNumber and StudentNotFound code per FR-04.
Run five closed sessions marking one imported student Absent in four sessions.
Close sixth session marking the same student Absent and query absence-threshold alert for that student.
cases.expected: FR-04 enrollment import establishes active enrollments (four accepted, one rejected with row-level error); only enrolled students participate in absence-rate calculation; target student at 83% unexcused absence rate triggers FR-26 alert after session close.

cases.title: POST /v1/auth/login as Lecturer then GET /v1/me before querying scoped absence-threshold alerts
preconditions: Lecturer test account assigned to class section with at-risk student exceeding absenceThresholdPercent 20.
Absence-threshold alert event exists for the student in the assigned section.
Student in a different section also has a threshold alert (out of lecturer scope).
steps: Call POST /v1/auth/login with Lecturer credentials.
Assert HTTP 200 with accessToken and Lecturer role in data envelope per FR-36.
Call GET /v1/me with Bearer token; assert assigned section scope in data.
Call GET /v1/alerts/absence-threshold?classSectionId={assignedSectionId} with Lecturer Authorization.
Assert HTTP 200 includes at-risk student for assigned section only; query with unassigned classSectionId returns HTTP 403 OutOfScope.
cases.expected: Auth gate per FR-15 and FR-36 succeeds; Lecturer retrieves FR-26 absence-threshold alerts only for assigned sections; cross-section alert data is denied without leakage.

cases.title: GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance roster includes atRisk flag when student exceeds absence threshold
preconditions: Open session for class section; one enrolled student has unexcused absence rate above effective absenceThresholdPercent 20.
Prior absence-threshold alert already evaluated for the at-risk student.
Lecturer bearer token is assigned to the session section.
steps: Call GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance with Lecturer Authorization.
Assert HTTP 200 standard envelope with data.rows array.
Locate at-risk student row and verify atRisk or thresholdExceeded boolean true with unexcusedAbsenceRate and absenceThresholdPercent fields.
Verify non-at-risk student rows have atRisk false or field absent.
Confirm data.counts (present, late, pending) remain accurate per FR-19 roster shape.
cases.expected: Live roster payload per FR-19 surfaces FR-26 threshold state: at-risk student row includes threshold breach indicator and computed rate without altering attendance status counts; roster shape and counts remain consistent with existing attendance API contract.

cases.title: WF close-session lifecycle triggers absent finalization then student and lecturer absence-threshold notifications
preconditions: WF-01 complete: section, enrollments, policy absenceThresholdPercent 20, multiple sessions Scheduled.
Lecturer completes WF-02 open/close cycles leaving target student with repeated Absent outcomes.
M10 notification delivery queue captures student, lecturer, and academic-admin recipients per policy defaults.
steps: Run sequential session open/close cycles until target student unexcused absence rate exceeds 20%.
On threshold-crossing close, verify BR-13 Absent applied for pending student in that session.
Query notification queue for three recipient types: student self, section lecturer, academic admin.
Confirm each notification payload includes section code, student identifier, computed rate, and threshold percent.
cases.expected: Primary WF close-session happy path produces BR-17 informational notifications to student, lecturer, and academic admin per FR-26 when threshold is exceeded; attendance ledger reflects only Absent/Present/Late statuses from close processing, not alert side effects.

cases.title: Student remains below threshold until Nth absent session then alert fires on crossing session only
preconditions: Section policy absenceThresholdPercent 20; ten total sessions planned.
Student starts with zero Absent; each absent session is 10% rate increment.
steps: Close sessions 1–2 with student Absent (20% rate — at threshold boundary).
Verify no absence-threshold alert emitted at exactly 20% if BR-17 requires strict exceed (>).
Close session 3 with student Absent (30% rate — exceeds 20%).
Verify alert emits on session 3 close only.
Close session 4 with student Present; verify absence rate recalculates but alert deduplication prevents duplicate notification if still above threshold.
cases.expected: Alternate branch: alert fires only when rate strictly exceeds absenceThresholdPercent (not merely equals); threshold crossing on third absent session triggers first alert; subsequent closes while still above threshold do not spam duplicate alerts.

cases.title: Raising absenceThresholdPercent via policy PATCH suppresses alert on next evaluation for borderline student
preconditions: Student unexcused absence rate 22% with policy absenceThresholdPercent 20; alert already emitted.
AcademicAdmin authenticated; section-scoped policy row exists.
Audit query available for policy mutation.
steps: Record beforeState: student rate 22%, threshold 20%, alert active.
AcademicAdmin PATCH /v1/policies/{policyId} changing absenceThresholdPercent from 20 to 25.
Close another session (student Present) and invoke M10 re-evaluation.
Record afterState: rate ~20% (22% of 10 sessions if one more held), threshold 25%.
Query audit logs for policy mutation and verify no new absence-threshold alert for previously alerted student.
cases.expected: Critical policy configuration change updates threshold for subsequent BR-17 evaluations; student at 22% no longer exceeds 25% threshold; no new alert emitted; policy mutation audit captured per BR-22; prior attendance records unchanged.

cases.title: Student denied cross-student absence-threshold alerts; Lecturer denied unassigned section alerts
preconditions: Two class sections A and B each have students exceeding absence threshold.
Student S enrolled only in section A; Lecturer L assigned only to section A.
Valid bearer tokens for Student S, Lecturer L, and Lecturer L2 (assigned to section B only).
steps: Call GET /v1/alerts/absence-threshold?studentUserId={studentInSectionB} as Student S.
Assert HTTP 403 Forbidden; no other student's rate or alert metadata returned.
Call GET /v1/alerts/absence-threshold?classSectionId={sectionB} as Lecturer L (assigned to section A only).
Assert HTTP 403 OutOfScope.
Call GET /v1/alerts/absence-threshold?classSectionId={sectionA} as Lecturer L; assert HTTP 200 with scoped results only.
cases.expected: RBAC enforces self-only alert read for Student and section-scoped read for Lecturer per BR-19; cross-student and cross-section absence-threshold data denied without partial leakage.

cases.title: Unexcused rate exactly at absenceThresholdPercent does not emit alert; one additional Absent exceeds and emits
preconditions: Section policy absenceThresholdPercent 20.00.
Student with exactly 20% unexcused absence rate (e.g. 2 Absent of 10 closed sessions).
M10 evaluator invoked after each session close.
steps: Invoke M10 evaluateAbsenceThreshold at exactly 20.00% computed rate.
Assert no alert event persisted (BR-17 condition is exceeds, not meets).
Close one additional session with student Absent yielding 27.27% rate (3 of 11).
Re-invoke evaluator and assert alert event created with rate and threshold in payload.
cases.expected: Boundary enforces strict exceed semantics: 20.00% against 20% threshold produces no alert; crossing to 27.27% triggers BR-17 alert generation.

cases.title: Null or undefined absenceThresholdPercent in effective policy disables alert generation
preconditions: Effective policy resolved for section has absenceThresholdPercent null (field undefined at all precedence levels).
Student with high unexcused absence rate (e.g. 50% Absent).
M10 notification module enabled globally.
steps: Call M06 resolveEffectivePolicy(classSectionId, now) and confirm absenceThresholdPercent is null.
Invoke M10 evaluateAbsenceThreshold(classSectionId, studentUserId) after session close.
Query alert events table for the student-section pair.
Verify no alert row created and no error thrown.
cases.expected: When effective policy omits absenceThresholdPercent, FR-26 alert evaluation is skipped gracefully; no false alerts for institutions that have not configured a threshold; attendance processing unaffected.

cases.title: Student attendance history page shows warning alert when unexcused absence rate exceeds policy threshold
preconditions: Student logged in on mobile-web Chromium at personal attendance history route.
Student enrolled in section where unexcused absence rate exceeds absenceThresholdPercent 20.
Absence-threshold alert has been generated for the student-section pair.
steps: Navigate to student attendance history for the at-risk section.
Verify section summary card displays unexcused absence rate percentage and configured threshold.
Observe warning alert component (semantic warning styling per alerts.md) with localized copy explaining threshold breach.
Confirm alert is informational only: no attendance status rows are editable by the student.
Verify other students' data is not visible on the page.
cases.expected: Student UI journey surfaces FR-26 threshold breach as a warning alert on personal attendance history per FR-37 self-scope; rate and threshold values match backend calculation; alert does not expose other students' records.

cases.title: Lecturer section dashboard highlights at-risk students with absence-threshold badge
preconditions: Lecturer logged in on desktop Chromium at assigned section dashboard.
At least one enrolled student exceeds absenceThresholdPercent 20; others remain below threshold.
Section has upcoming and past sessions listed per FR-10.
steps: Navigate to class section detail or session list for the assigned section.
Locate student roster or section summary showing per-student absence rate.
Verify at-risk student row displays threshold-exceeded badge (warning variant per badges.md) with rate and threshold tooltip.
Verify below-threshold students show no threshold badge.
Click at-risk student row and confirm detail panel shows alert timestamp and informational message without edit controls.
cases.expected: Lecturer browser journey satisfies FR-26 visibility: at-risk students are visually distinguished on section dashboard per FR-10 listing patterns; badge reflects BR-17 threshold breach; non-at-risk students remain unbadged; data scoped to lecturer-assigned section only.
